observations.principle: A presentation product launch should show useful starting points, not only a polished hero claim.
observations.code_generation_rule: Create editable slide patterns with reusable titles, labels, panels, and proof objects so the deck demonstrates a system users can modify.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy source templates, screenshots, layouts, brand marks, or product visuals.

observations.principle: A product-event narrative works best when a broad workflow shift frames the feature introduction.
observations.code_generation_rule: Use editable sequence slides that move from problem to shift to workflow before naming implementation details.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy source prose, keynote staging, screenshots, or launch layouts.

observations.principle: A flexible identity can stretch across dense and expressive surfaces when it is based on a small set of primitives.
observations.code_generation_rule: Use editable primitive shapes, repeated proof badges, and consistent spacing tokens that can vary by slide pattern.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy Figma glyphs, event identity, colors, photography, layouts, or brand marks.

observations.principle: Expressive marks should support comprehension and should recede on dense information slides.
observations.code_generation_rule: Keep editable decorative primitives sparse on text-heavy slides and reserve signal treatments for proof objects and transitions.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy source glyph placement, event surfaces, template files, or visual motifs.

observations.principle: Dense AI capability becomes commercially understandable when it is broken into named steps and visible outcomes.
observations.code_generation_rule: Build editable workflow diagrams with short step labels, clear connectors, and one outcome panel per process.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy Supervity visuals, flow layouts, palette, mockups, or case-study diagrams.

observations.principle: High-stakes launch decks need proof objects that make abstract product promises inspectable.
observations.code_generation_rule: Add editable proof objects such as file maps, artifact grids, score matrices, and QA gates instead of long text blocks.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy source screenshots, proprietary assets, full prose, or branded proof examples.

observations.principle: The review workflow is part of the product value when teams need reliable launch-grade decks.
observations.code_generation_rule: Represent review as editable stages for contact sheet, layout JSON, Gemini critique, repair pass, and human approval.
observations.do_not_copy: Do not copy source event workflows, agency process graphics, screenshots, or brand marks.
